content_en:
Al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Quran — The Proof in the Quranic Sciences — was composed by az-Zarkashi as the first comprehensive systematic treatment of the disciplines of ulum al-Quran. Before az-Zarkashi, there had been works on individual Quranic sciences — works on qira'at (readings), on asbab an-nuzul (occasions of revelation), on nasikh and mansukh (abrogation) — but no single work had attempted to bring all of these sciences together into a unified framework. Al-Burhan was the first such attempt, and its organization and coverage became the template that as-Suyuti used when composing al-Itqan.

Az-Zarkashi's methodology is systematic and scholarly. For each of the sciences he addresses, he identifies the major authorities who have written on that topic, surveys their positions, presents the relevant evidence, and draws conclusions. His approach is critical rather than merely compilatory: he evaluates the reliability of reports about the circumstances of revelation, the strength of arguments about abrogation, and the validity of claims about the Quran's linguistic features. This critical dimension distinguishes al-Burhan from mere encyclopedic compilation.

The work is organized into forty-seven types (naw') of Quranic knowledge. These range from the revelation of the Quran and its descent, to its collection and writing, to its recitation and modes, to its linguistic and rhetorical features, to its interpretation and the sciences of tafsir. The coverage is not identical to al-Itqan — as-Suyuti added material and reorganized some sections — but the overall framework is similar enough that the two works can be usefully compared to see how the field developed in the half century between them.

Az-Zarkashi shows particular strength in the linguistic and rhetorical dimensions of Quranic sciences. His chapters on the language of the Quran, its rhetorical figures, its grammatical features, and its engagement with the Arabic linguistic tradition of the time of revelation reflect his deep grounding in Arabic linguistics and literary criticism.
content_id:
Al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Qur'an — Bukti dalam Ilmu-ilmu Al-Qur'an — disusun oleh al-Zarkasyi sebagai perlakuan sistematis komprehensif pertama terhadap disiplin-disiplin ulum al-Qur'an. Sebelum al-Zarkasyi, sudah ada karya-karya tentang ilmu-ilmu Al-Qur'an individual — karya-karya tentang qiraat (bacaan), asbab al-nuzul (sebab-sebab turun wahyu), nasikh dan mansukh (nasakh) — namun tidak ada satu karya pun yang mencoba menyatukan semua ilmu-ilmu ini ke dalam satu kerangka yang terpadu. Al-Burhan adalah upaya pertama semacam itu, dan organisasi serta cakupannya menjadi template yang al-Suyuthi gunakan ketika menyusun al-Itqan.

Metodologi al-Zarkasyi bersifat sistematis dan keilmuan. Untuk setiap ilmu yang ia bahas, ia mengidentifikasi para otoritas utama yang telah menulis tentang topik tersebut, mensurvei posisi-posisi mereka, menyajikan bukti-bukti yang relevan, dan menarik kesimpulan-kesimpulan. Pendekatannya bersifat kritis, bukan sekadar kompilatoris: ia mengevaluasi keandalan laporan-laporan tentang keadaan-keadaan turun wahyu, kekuatan argumen-argumen tentang nasakh, dan validitas klaim-klaim tentang fitur-fitur linguistik Al-Qur'an. Dimensi kritis ini membedakan al-Burhan dari sekadar kompilasi ensiklopedis.

Karya ini disusun dalam empat puluh tujuh jenis (naw') pengetahuan Al-Qur'an. Jenis-jenis ini berkisar dari turunnya Al-Qur'an dan pewahyuannya, pengumpulan dan penulisannya, cara bacaan dan ragamnya, fitur-fitur linguistik dan retorisnya, hingga penafsiran dan ilmu-ilmu tafsir. Cakupannya tidak identik dengan al-Itqan — al-Suyuthi menambahkan materi dan menyusun ulang beberapa bagian — namun kerangka keseluruhannya cukup serupa sehingga kedua karya dapat dibandingkan secara berguna untuk melihat bagaimana bidang ini berkembang dalam setengah abad di antara keduanya.

Al-Zarkasyi menunjukkan kekuatan khusus dalam dimensi-dimensi linguistik dan retoris ilmu-ilmu Al-Qur'an. Bab-babnya tentang bahasa Al-Qur'an, figur-figur retorisnya, fitur-fitur gramatikalnya, dan keterlibatannya dengan tradisi linguistik Arab pada masa turunnya wahyu mencerminkan landasannya yang mendalam dalam linguistik Arab dan kritik sastra.
content_ar: Al-Burhan fi Ulum al-القرآن الكريم — The Proof in the القرآني Sciences — was composed by az-Zarkashi as the أولاً comprehensive systematic treatment of the disciplines of ulum al-القرآن الكريم. قبل az-Zarkashi, there had been works on individual القرآني sciences — works on qira'at (readings), on asbab an-nuzul (occasions of الوحي), on nasikh and mansukh (abrogation) — but no single work had attempted to bring all of these sciences together into a unified framework. Al-Burhan was the أولاً such attempt, and its organization and coverage became the template that as-Suyuti used عندما composing al-Itqan. Az-Zarkashi's methodology is systematic and العلمي. For each of the sciences he addresses, he identifies the major authorities who have written on that topic, surveys their positions, presents the relevant evidence, and draws conclusions. His approach is البالغ الأهمية rather than merely compilatory: he evaluates the reliability of reports about the circumstances of الوحي, the القوة of arguments about abrogation, and the validity of claims about the القرآن الكريم's linguistic features. This البالغ الأهمية dimension distinguishes al-Burhan from mere encyclopedic compilation. The work is organized into forty-seven types (naw') of القرآني العلم. These range from the الوحي of the القرآن الكريم and its descent, to its collection and writing, to its التلاوة and modes, to its linguistic and rhetorical features, to its interpretation and the sciences of التفسير. The coverage is not identical to al-Itqan — as-Suyuti added material and reorganized some sections — but the overall framework is similar enough that the two works can be usefully compared to see how the field developed in the half century between them. Az-Zarkashi shows particular القوة in the linguistic and rhetorical dimensions of القرآني sciences. His الأبواب on the language of the القرآن الكريم, its rhetorical figures, its grammatical features, and its engagement with the Arabic linguistic التراث of the time of الوحي reflect his deep grounding in Arabic linguistics and literary criticism.
